title: Mellow Korean Shrimp Stew
description: A jammy, velvety stew combining dried shrimp, fresh shrimp, and coconut milk for a cozy Korean-American dinner.
instructions: Rinse the dried shrimps in cold water and set aside.
Peel and devein the fresh shrimp, reserving the tails for garnish.
Heat sesame oil in a pot over medium heat and sauté ginger and garlic until fragrant.
Add the dried shrimps and stir until they turn pink.
Pour in the coconut milk and water, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Stir in doenjang and gochujang until fully dissolved.
Add sliced carrots, mushrooms, and zucchini, simmering for 5 minutes.
Fold in the fresh shrimp and cook for an additional 3 minutes until opaque.
Season with salt to taste and simmer for 1 more minute.
Ladle into bowls, garnish with shrimp tails, and serve hot.
